item.title: Lies, Damned Lies, and Cybersecurity Metrics
item.description:
A panel of five C-suite leaders discuss how cybersecurity success is measured and why it isn't improving results.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Human vs AI: Debates Shape RSAC 2026 Cybersecurity Trends
item.description:
As AI dominated RSAC 2026, CISOs and industry leaders debated its role in security, from agentic applications to the challenges of scaling human involvement in decision-making.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: RSAC 2026: How AI Is Reshaping Cybersecurity Faster Than Ever
item.description:
Dark Reading's Kelly Jackson Higgins shares insights on the past, present, and future of cybersecurity after attending RSAC 2026 Conference.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Grafana Patches AI Bug That Could Have Leaked User Data
item.description:
By hiding malicious instructions on an attacker-controlled Web page, AI could ingest orders as benign and return sensitive data to the attacker's server.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Storm-1175 Deploys Medusa Ransomware at 'High Velocity'
item.description:
Microsoft says the financially motivated cybercrime group has exploited N-day and zero-day vulnerabilities in campaigns predicated on speed.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Medusa Ransomware Fast to Exploit Vulnerabilities, Breached Systems
item.description:
The group is using zero-days, quickly weaponizes fresh bugs, and exfiltrates and encrypts data within days of initial access. The post Medusa Ransomware Fast to Exploit Vulnerabilities, Breached Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: GPUBreach: Root Shell Access Achieved via GPU Rowhammer Attack
item.description:
Researchers have demonstrated that GPU Rowhammer attacks can be used to escalate privileges. The post GPUBreach: Root Shell Access Achieved via GPU Rowhammer Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: GrafanaGhost: Attackers Can Abuse Grafana to Leak Enterprise Data
item.description:
By targeting Grafana’s AI components, attackers can point to external resources and inject indirect prompts to bypass safeguards. The post GrafanaGhost: Attackers Can Abuse Grafana to Leak Enterprise Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Severe StrongBox Vulnerability Patched in Android
item.description:
A critical DoS vulnerability in the Framework component of Android has also been fixed with the latest update. The post Severe StrongBox Vulnerability Patched in Android appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Flowise Vulnerability in Attacker Crosshairs
item.description:
The improper validation of user-supplied JavaScript code allows attackers to execute arbitrary code and access the file system. The post Critical Flowise Vulnerability in Attacker Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: The New Rules of Engagement: Matching Agentic Attack Speed
item.description:
The cybersecurity response to AI-enabled nation-state threats cannot be incremental. It must be architectural. The post The New Rules of Engagement: Matching Agentic Attack Speed appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Anthropic Unveils ‘Claude Mythos’ – A Cybersecurity Breakthrough That Could Also Supercharge Attacks
item.description:
New AI model drives Project Glasswing, a effort to secure critical software before advanced capabilities fall into the wrong hands. The post Anthropic Unveils ‘Claude Mythos’ – A Cybersecurity Breakthrough That Could Also Supercharge Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via PLC Attacks
item.description:
Federal agencies warn attackers are manipulating PLC and SCADA systems across multiple sectors, triggering operational disruptions and raising concerns over broader OT targeting. The post Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via PLC Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cyberattack hits Northern Ireland’s centralized school network, disrupting access for thousands
item.description:
The Education Authority (EA), which oversees school support services in Northern Ireland, said in an official statement it became aware of the incident affecting the “C2K” system last week and took immediate steps to contain the breach.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: UK exposes Russian cyber unit hacking home routers to hijack internet traffic
item.description:
Officials said the activity centers on compromising small office and home office routers and similar network devices exposed to the internet, often because of weak security settings or outdated software.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Massachusetts hospital turning ambulances away after cyberattack
item.description:
Signature Healthcare and Signature Healthcare Brockton Hospital said on Monday that the cyber incident is impacting many of their information systems.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: National security veterans warn against delays in FISA 702 reauthorization
item.description:
The missive, obtained by Recorded Future News, arrives days before policymakers return from recess next week and aim for a quick extension Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) for another 18 months.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FBI, Pentagon warn of Iran hacking groups targeting operational technology
item.description:
The advisory said Iranian actors are targeting local municipal governments, water and wastewater systems and the energy sector.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description:
An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description:
Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals
item.description:
Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital (NMH), a 226-bed hospital in Nacogdoches, Texas, has recently announced a data security incident that was first […] The post Nacogdoches Memorial Hospital Data Breach More Than 257,000 Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement
item.description:
Eye Physicians of Central Florida has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach that […] The post Eye Physicians of Central Florida Data Breach Settlement appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Iowa Attorney General Brenna Bird has filed a lawsuit against Change Healthcare, UnitedHealth Group, and Optum over the February 2024 […] The post Iowa AG Sues Change Healthcare Over 2024 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
Cardiovascular Consultants in Arizona has settled a class action lawsuit stemming from a 2023 data breach involving the protected health […] The post Cardiovascular Consultants Pays $3.85M to Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Stryker Fully Operational After March Cyberattack
item.description:
Stryker has announced that it has recovered systems impacted by its March 11 cyberattack and is once again fully operational […] The post Stryker Fully Operational After March Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Telehealth Giant Him & Hers Announces Data Breach
item.description:
The direct-to-consumer telehealth company Him & Hers has experienced a data breach. In early February, an unauthorized third party gained […] The post Telehealth Giant Him & Hers Announces Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: ProxyCare; Oscar Health; AccentCare Announce Data Breaches
item.description:
Data incidents have recently been announced by ProxyCare in Florida, Oscar Health in New York, and AccentCare in Texas. ProxyCare, […] The post ProxyCare; Oscar Health; AccentCare Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Researchers didn’t want to glamorize cybercrims. So they roasted them.
item.description:
Jessica Lyons reports: Cybercrime crews have become almost mystical entities, with security vendors assigning them names like Wizard Spider and Velvet Tempest. They hide out in hidden corners of the dark web (often accompanied by a clearnet leak site), leading some infosec folks to talk about these miscreants as if they are invincible. But not... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description:
Brian Krebs reports: An elusive hacker who went by the handle “UNKN” and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two data security incidents affected immigration law firms and their clients
item.description:
DocketWise is an immigration and case management solution designed for immigration attorneys. The firm informed the Maine Attorney General’s Office on April 3 of a September 1, 2025, data breach that affected the personal information of its law firm clients’ clients. The types of information involved varied by individual client. Their notification to Maine states... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: NYS school data incidents rose 72% in 2025, with 44 reported on Long Island
item.description:
Lorena Mongelli reports: Reports of compromised student data and cybersecurity in schools surged statewide in 2025, according to education officials. Statewide, data incident reports rose 72%, from 384 in 2024 to 662 in 2025, an annual report issued by the state Education Department’s chief privacy officer found. On Long Island, schools reported 44 data incidents in 2025, a jump from 35 the year prior, according to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Two Breaches, One Quarter: Valley Family Health Care’s Challenging Start to 2026
item.description:
On January 12, Valley Family Health Care (VFHC) notified HHS after learning that the TriZetto Provider Solutions (TPS) breach had affected 4,300 of their patients. The TPS breach, which began in November 2024, involved their patients’ names, addresses, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, health insurance member numbers (including Medicare beneficiary identifiers), health insurer names,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
item.description:
Sergiu Gatlan reports: Microsoft says that Storm-1175, a China-based financially motivated cybercriminal group known for deploying Medusa ransomware payloads, has been deploying n-day and zero-day exploits in high-velocity attacks. This cybercrime gang quickly shifts to targeting new security vulnerabilities to gain access to its victims’ networks, weaponizing some of them within a day and, in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Maine House advances McCabe bill to strengthen cybersecurity at Maine hospitals
item.description:
A press release on April 6, 2026 from Maine House Democrats: On Thursday, the Maine House voted unanimously to advance a bill from Rep. Julie McCabe, D-Lewiston, that would help prevent cybersecurity attacks on Maine hospitals and ensure continuity of patient care when future cyberattacks occur. As amended, LD 2103 would require Maine hospitals to adopt a... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Jones Day confirms limited breach after phishing attack by Silent Ransom Group
item.description:
One of the top-ranked law firms in the country confirmed today that it has suffered a data breach. Jones Day disclosed the breach after hackers known as Silent Ransom Group (SRG) posted the data ​to their dark web leak site on March 30. A spokesperson for the firm said that limited files for 10 clients... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: 1 Billion Microsoft Users Warned As Angry Hacker Drops 0-Day Exploit
item.description:
Davey Winder reports: Usually, when I report zero-day exploits, it’s because attacks by threat actors are already underway or a vendor has released a patch after becoming aware of the vulnerability. BlueHammer, however, is different. This time, it’s a security researcher who has released the Windows attack exploit code; there is no patch available, and... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Iranian-Affiliated Cyber Actors Exploit Programmable Logic Controllers Across US Critical Infrastructure
item.description:
From: CISA Date: April 7, 2026 Alert Code: AA26-097A Executive Summary: Iran-affiliated advanced persistent threat (APT) actors are conducting exploitation activity targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices, including programmable logic controllers (PLCs) manufactured by Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley. This activity has led to PLC disruptions across several U.S. critical infrastructure sectors through malicious interactions with the project... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to QILIN have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...

item.title: Microsoft links Medusa ransomware affiliate to zero-day attacks
item.description:
Microsoft says that Storm-1175, a China-based financially motivated cybercriminal group known for deploying Medusa ransomware payloads, has been deploying n-day and zero-day exploits in high-velocity attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Disgruntled researcher leaks “BlueHammer” Windows zero-day exploit
item.description:
Exploit code has been released for an unpatched Windows privilege escalation flaw reported privately to Microsoft, allowing attackers to gain SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: New GPUBreach attack enables system takeover via GPU rowhammer
item.description:
A new attack, dubbed GPUBreach, can induce Rowhammer bit-flips on GPU GDDR6 memories to escalate privileges and lead to a full system compromise. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: German authorities identify REvil and GandCrab ransomware bosses
item.description:
The Federal Police in Germany (BKA) has identified two Russian nationals as the leaders of GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations between 2019 and 2021. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Max severity Flowise RCE vulnerability now exploited in attacks
item.description:
Hackers are exploiting a maximum-severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-59528, in the open-source platform Flowise for building custom LLM apps and agentic systems to execute arbitrary code. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: US warns of Iranian hackers targeting critical infrastructure
item.description:
Iranian-linked hackers are targeting Internet-exposed Rockwell/Allen-Bradley programmable logic controllers (PLCs) on the networks of U.S. critical infrastructure organizations. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Snowflake customers hit in data theft attacks after SaaS integrator breach
item.description:
Over a dozen companies have suffered data theft attacks after a SaaS integration provider was breached and authentication tokens stolen. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers exploit critical flaw in Ninja Forms WordPress plugin
item.description:
A critical vulnerability in the Ninja Forms File Uploads premium add-on for WordPress allows uploading arbitrary files without authentication, which can lead to remote code execution. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: TIMEC Oil & Gas, Inc.
item.description:
